prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. An ongoing refugee crisis began in Europe in late February 2022 after Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Over 8.1 million refugees fleeing Ukraine have been recorded across Europe, while an estimated 8 million others had been displaced within the country by late May 2022. Approximately one-quarter of the country's total population had left their homes in Ukraine by 20 March. 90% of Ukrainian refugees are women and children, while most Ukrainian men age 18 to 60 are banned from leaving the country. By 24 March, more than half of all children in Ukraine had left their homes, of whom a quarter had left the country. The invasion caused Europe's largest refugee crisis since World War II and its aftermath, is the first of its kind in Europe since the
B. The invasion of Ukraine by Russia in late February 2022 caused an ongoing refugee crisis in Europe. Over 8.1 million refugees have been recorded across the country, and approximately 8 million others had been displaced within the country by late May 2022. About one-quarter of the country's total population had left their homes in Ukraine by 20 March. Women and children make up 90% of Ukrainian refugees, while most men aged 18 to 60 are prohibited from leaving the country. By 24 March, more than half of all children in Ukraine had left their homes, of whom a quarter had left the country.
C. Europe experienced a continuous refugee crisis starting in late February 2022, following the invasion of Ukraine by Russia.  More than 8.1 million Ukrainian refugees have been documented throughout Europe. Concurrently, an estimated 8 million individuals had been internally displaced within Ukraine by the end of May 2022. By March 20th, roughly one-fourth of the nation's entire populace had departed their residences in Ukraine. A significant majority, 90%, of Ukrainian refugees consist of women and children. This is largely due to the fact that most Ukrainian men between the ages of 18 and 60 are prohibited from exiting the country. As of March 24th, over 50% of all children residing in Ukraine had evacuated their homes, with 25% of those children having left the country entirely. This invasion triggered Europe's most substantial refugee crisis since the period of World War II and its immediate aftermath, representing the first such event in Europe since the.

Answer:

By 24 March, more than half of all children in Ukraine had left their homes, of whom a quarter had left the country. About one-quarter of the country's total population had left their homes in Ukraine by 20 March. By 24 March, more than half of all children in Ukraine had left their homes, of whom a quarter had left the country.
options.C: Europe experienced a continuous refugee crisis starting in late February 2022, following the invasion of Ukraine by Russia.  More than 8.1 million Ukrainian refugees have been documented throughout Europe. Concurrently, an estimated 8 million individuals had been internally displaced within Ukraine by the end of May 2022.
